text: The values of x at the stationary points of f(x) = x³ + 3x² - 2 are
explanation:
Let y = f(x) = x³ + 3x² - 2
\(\frac{dy}{dx} = 3x^2 + 6x\)
\(\frac{d^2y}{dx^2} = 6x + 6\)
For stationary points \(\frac{dy}{dx} = 0\)
\(\Rightarrow 3x(x + 2) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 0, -2\).
name: Question Item 1

text: The function f(x) = (1/2)^x on R is
explanation:
Let y = (1/2)^x on R
\(\frac{dy}{dx} = \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^x \log \frac{1}{2} = -\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^x \log 2\)
(\(\frac{dy}{dx} < 0\)) so strictly decreasing.
name: Question Item 2

text: The slope of the tangent of the curve \(\left(\frac{x}{31}\right)^n + \left(\frac{y}{1209}\right)^n = 2\) at (31, 1209) ............
explanation:
\(\left(\frac{x}{31}\right)^n + \left(\frac{y}{1209}\right)^n = 2\)
On differentiating
\(\Rightarrow \frac{nx^{n-1}}{31^n} + \frac{ny^{n-1}}{1209^n} \frac{dy}{dx} = 0\)
\(\Rightarrow \frac{dy}{dx} = -\left(\frac{x}{y} ight)^{n-1} \times (39)^n\)
Slope of tangent at (31, 1209)
\(= -\left(\frac{1}{39} ight)^{n-1} \times (39)^n = -39\).
name: Question Item 3

text: If y = 5x² + 6x + 6, x = 2, Δx = 0.001, then value of dy is
name: Question Item 4

text: A man 2 m tall, walks at the rate of 1/3 m/s towards a street light which is 5/3 m above the ground. The rate at which the length of his shadow is changing when he is 3/3 m away from the base of the light is ......
explanation:
From ΔABE and ΔDCE,
\(\frac{AB}{CD} = \frac{AE}{CE} = \frac{AC + CE}{CE}\)
\(\Rightarrow \frac{AB}{CD} = \frac{AC}{CE} + 1 \Rightarrow \frac{AC}{CE} = \frac{AB}{CD} - 1\)
\(\Rightarrow \frac{AC}{CE} = \frac{5}{3} - 1 = \frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow CE = \frac{3}{5}AC\)
Differentiating with respect to time we get,
\(\frac{d}{dt}CE = \frac{3}{5}\left(\frac{d}{dt}AC\right)\)
\(\frac{d}{dt}AC = -1\frac{2}{3} \text{ ms}^{-1} \Rightarrow \frac{d}{dt}AC = -\frac{5}{3} \text{ ms}^{-1}\)
Rate of change of shadow length
\(= \frac{3}{5}\left(-\frac{5}{3}\right) = -1 \text{ ms}^{-1}\).
name: Question Item 5

text: The equation of tangent to the curve y = 5x² - 3x + 7 at the point (-1, 4) is .......
explanation:
Curve is, y = 5x² - 3x + 7
Slope of tangent to curve at (-1, 4) is,
\(m_T = \left.\frac{dy}{dx}\right|_{(-1, 4)} = (10x - 3)\big|_{x = -1} = -13\)
Equation of tangent is,
\(y - 4 = -13(x + 1)\)
\(\Rightarrow 13x + y + 9 = 0\).
name: Question Item 6

text: A kind of bacteria grows by t³ in t s. Time taken for the rate of growth of the bacteria to become 1200 per s is ......
explanation:
Given,
\(\frac{dN}{dt} = t^3,\)
When, rate of growth is 1200 we have,
\(\frac{d}{dt}\left(\frac{dN}{dt}\right) = 1200 \Rightarrow \frac{d}{dt}t^3 = 1200\)
\(\Rightarrow 3t^2 = 1200 \Rightarrow t^2 = 400\)
\(\Rightarrow t = 20 \text{ sec}\).
name: Question Item 7

text: The sides of an equilateral triangle are increasing at the rate of 2 cm s⁻¹. How fast does its area increase when its side is 10 cm?
explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle \(A = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^2\).
\(\frac{dA}{dt} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} a \frac{da}{dt}\).
When a=10 cm and da/dt=2 cm/s,
\(\frac{dA}{dt} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 10 \times 2 = 10\sqrt{3}\) cm²/s.
name: Question Item 8

text: The curve 3y² = 2ax² + 6b passes through the point P(3, -1) and the gradient of the curve at P is “-1”, then the values of a and b are
explanation:
Equation of given curve is
\(3y^2 = 2ax^2 + 6b \quad \ldots(i)\)
∵ Curve (i) passes through point P(3, -1), so
\(3 = 18a + 6b\)
\(\Rightarrow 6a + 2b = 1 \quad \ldots(ii)\)
On differentiating the Eq. (i), we get
\(6y\frac{dy}{dx} = 4ax\)
\(\Rightarrow \quad 6 \times (-1) \times (-1) = 4a(3) \Rightarrow a = 1/2\)
\(\therefore b = -1\).
name: Question Item 9

text: The value of 'k' for which the function f(x) = k(x + sin x) + k is increasing, is equal to
explanation:
Given function f(x) = k(x + sin x) + k
So, f'(x) = k(1 + cos x)
∵ f(x) is an increasing function, so
f'(x) ≥ 0
\(\Rightarrow \quad k(1 + \cos x) \geq 0\)
\(\Rightarrow \quad k > 0 \quad \{\because 1 + \cos x \geq 0\}\).
name: Question Item 10

text: Find the equation of the normal to the curve y = (x - 7)/((x - 2)(x - 3)) at the point where it cuts the X-axis.
explanation:
Given curve, y = (x - 7)/((x - 2)(x - 3)), cuts the X-axis at point P(7,0).
Now, dy/dx = [(x - 2)(x - 3) - (x - 7)[(x - 2) + (x - 3)]] / [(x - 2)²(x - 3)²]
∴ dy/dx|_{x=7} = 1/20
So, the slope of normal to given curve at point P is -20.
Therefore equation of required normal is
y - 0 = -20(x - 7) ⇒ 20x + y - 140 = 0.
name: Question Item 11

text: The curves y = 4x² + 2x - 8 and y = x³ - x + 13 touch each other at the point
explanation:
y = 4x² + 2x - 8
differentiate w.r.t. 'x' on both sides,
dy/dx = 8x + 2
y = x³ - x + 13
differentiate w.r.t. 'x' on both sides,
dy/dx = 3x² - 1
Since, curves are touch each other
⇒ 8x + 2 = 3x² - 1
⇒ 3x² - 8x - 3 = 0
⇒ (x - 3)(3x + 1) = 0
⇒ x = 3 (or) -1/3
Put, x = 3 in y = x³ - x + 13
y = 37
∴ Point of contact P = (3, 37).
name: Question Item 12

text: If the subnormal at any point on the curve yⁿ = ax is constant then the value of n is
explanation:
Equation of given curve yⁿ = ax, so
ny^{n-1} dy/dx = a ⇒ dy/dx = (a/n) y^{1-n}
∴ The length of subnormal = |y dy/dx| = |y (a/n) y^{1-n}|
= |(a/n) y^{2-n}|
It is given that length of subnormal is constant, means it must be independent of x and y terms, so n = 2.
name: Question Item 13

text: If the radius of a sphere is mentioned as 7 m with an error of 0.02 m, then the approximate error in calculating its volume is
explanation:
Radius (r) = 7m
Error in radius (dr) = 0.02m
Volume of sphere (v) = (4/3)π r³
differentiate w.r.t. 'r' on both sides,
dv/dr = 4π r²
dv = 4π r² dr = 4π(49) · (0.02) = 3.92π m³.
name: Question Item 14

text: The tangent at the point (1, 2) to the curve y² = 4x makes an angle θ with the positive direction of X-axis. Then θ =
explanation:
y² = 4x; P = (1, 2)
differentiate w.r. to 'x'
2y · y' = 4 · 1 ⇒ y' = 4/(2y)
y' = 2/y
Slope of tangent = y'|_{P(1,2)} = 2/|y|_{P(1,2)}
tan θ = 1 ⇒ θ = 45°.
name: Question Item 15

text: The interval in which y = ln(ln(x)), x > 1 is decreasing is
explanation:
Given function y = ln(ln(x)), x > 1
dy/dx = 1/(x ln x), x > 1
∵ y is a decreasing, then
dy/dx < 0 ⇒ 1/(x ln x) < 0
∴ x > 1 ⇒ ln x < 0 ⇒ x < 1, which is not possible as x > 1
∴ x ∈ ∅ for decreasing.
name: Question Item 16

text: Maximum area of the rectangle that can be formed with the fixed perimeter 'p' cm ............
explanation:
Let length of adjacent sides of rectangle is x cm and y cm so perimeter of rectangle is p = 2(x + y) cm and the area A = xy cm²
⇒ A = x (p/2 - x)
for maxima dA/dx = 0 ⇒ p/2 - 2x = 0 ⇒ x = p/4 cm and y = p/4 cm
∴ For given perimeter of rectangle p, the maximum possible area A = (p/4) × (p/4) = p²/16 cm².
name: Question Item 17

text: Find the value of 'p' and 'q' if the function f(t) = t³ - 6t² + pt + q defined on [1, 3] satisfies the Rolle's theorem for c = (2√3 + 1)/√3
explanation:
It is given that, function f(t) = t³ - 6t² + pt + q defined on [1, 3] satisfied the Rolle's theorem, so
f(1) = f(3)
⇒ 1 - 6 + p + q = 27 - 54 + 3p + q
⇒ 2p = 22 ⇒ p = 11 and q ∈ ℝ.
name: Question Item 18

text: The equation of the normal to the curve y = sin x at the point (0, 0) is
explanation:
Given curve y = sin x
∴ Slope of normal to the curve at point (0, 0)
= - (dx/dy)|_{(0,0)} = - (1/cos x)|_{(0,0)} = -1
∴ Equation of normal to the curve at point (0, 0) is
y - 0 = -1(x - 0) ⇒ x + y = 0.
name: Question Item 19

text: Displacement 's' of a particle, in meters, at any time 't' s is expressed as s = t³/3 - 6t. Find the acceleration at a time when the velocity vanishes
explanation:
Given, displacement of a particle at time t as
s = t³/3 - 6t
∴ Velocity = ds/dt = t² - 6 = v
(let)
and as velocity vanishes, so v = 0 ⇒ t = √6 sec
Now, the acceleration a = dv/dt = 2t
∴ Acceleration a at t = √6 sec is 2√6 m/sec².
name: Question Item 20

text: The radius of a circle is increasing at a rate of 0.1 cm s⁻¹. Then the rate of change of area, when its radius is 5 cm, is
explanation:
Let the circle have radius r cm and area A cm² and it is given that dr/dt = 0.1 cm s⁻¹ and as we know area A = π r² ⇒ dA/dt = 2π r dr/dt
∴ Rate of change of area, when radius is 5 cm is
(dA/dt)|_{r = 5 cm} = 2π(5)(0.1) = π cm² · s⁻¹.
name: Question Item 21

text: What is an approximate value of √199 corrected to 4 decimal places?
name: Question Item 22

text: A point on the curve x = 3 cos θ, y = 2 sin θ at which the tangent is perpendicular to X-axis is
explanation: Given curve is x = 3 cos θ, y = 2 sin θ is represent ellipse, x²/9 + y²/4 = 1 and the tangent perpendicular to X-axis can be drawn at end points of the major axis means either at (3, 0) or (-3, 0).
name: Question Item 23

text: If displacement s = 5 sin(2t), then the velocity at the end of π/3 s is
explanation:
s = 5 sin(2t)
differentiate w.r.t. t
ds/dt = 5 · d/dt sin(2t)
ds/dt = 5 cos(2t) · 2 = 10 cos 2t
Velocity when t = π/3 sec = (ds/dt)|_{t = π/3} = 10 cos 2(π/3)
= 10 × -1/2 = -5.
name: Question Item 24

text: What is the value of ∛26 corrected up to three decimal places?
name: Question Item 25

text: Find the equation of normal to the curve y = x³ - 3x, which is parallel to the line 2x + 18y = 9?
explanation:
Given, line is 2x + 18y = 9
slope (m) = -a/b
m = -2/18 = -1/9
Given equation of curve is
y = x³ - 3x ...(i)
Differentiate w.r.t. 'x', we get
dy/dx = 3x² - 3
∴ Slope of normal = -1/(dy/dx) = -1/(3x² - 3)
Since, normal is parallel to given line
∴ Slope of line = slope of normal
-1/9 = -1/(3(x² - 1))
⇒ x² - 1 = 3 ⇒ x = ±2
Case (i) if x = 2
from Eq. (i)
y = (2)³ - 3(2)
y = 2
∴ Equation of normal is y - 2 = -1/9(x - 2)
x + 9y = 20
Case (ii) if x = -2
y = (-2)³ - 3(-2) = -2
∴ Q = (-2, -2)
Equation of Normal is y - y₁ = m(x - x₁)
y + 2 = -1/9(x + 2)
x + 9y = -20
Required Equation of normal is x + 9y = ±20.
name: Question Item 26

text: If the increase in the side of square is 6%, then the approximate percentage increase in its area ......
explanation:
Let side of square = x
Given, percentage change in side = 6%
dx/x × 100 = 6% ...(i)
We have, A = x²
Differentiate w.r.to 'x'
dA/dx = 2x ⇒ dA = 2x dx
Dividing by x² on both sides
dA/x² = 2 · (x dx)/x²
dA/A = 2 dx/x [∴ A = x²]
Now, multiply by 100
dA/A × 100 = 2 × (dx/x × 100)
Percentage change in Area = 2 × 6% [from Eq. (i)]
= 12%.
name: Question Item 27

text: Tangent at any point θ on the curve x = 35 sec θ, y = 35 tan θ is
options: y sin θ = x + 35 cos θ
y sin θ = x - 35 cos θ
y cos θ = x - 35 sin θ
y cos θ = x + 35 sin θ
explanation:
Given, x = 35 sec θ ...(i)
y = 35 tan θ ...(ii)
∴ Point P = (35 sec θ, 35 tan θ)
Differentiate Eq. (i) w.r.t to θ'
dx/dθ = 35 (sec θ · tan θ) ...(iii)
Differentiate Eq. (ii) w.r.t to θ'
dy/dθ = 35 (sec² θ) ...(iv)
⇒ Eq. (iv)/Eq. (iii) ⇒ dy/dx = [35 (sec² θ)] / [35 (sec θ · tan θ)]
dy/dx = sec θ / tan θ = 1/sin θ
∴ m = 1/sin θ
Equation of tangent is
y - 35 tan θ = m (x - 35 sec θ)
y - 35 sin θ / cos θ = 1/sin θ (x - 35 / cos θ)
y sin θ - 35 sin² θ / cos θ = x - 35 / cos θ
y sin θ = x + 35 (sin² θ - 1)/cos θ
y sin θ = x + 35 (-cos² θ)/cos θ
y sin θ = x - 35 cos θ.
name: Question Item 28

text: If y = (ax - b)/((x - 1)(x - 4)) has a turning point P(2, -1), then the values of a and b are
explanation:
Given, curve is y = (ax - b)/((x - 1)(x - 4)); P = (2, -1)
Since, P(2, -1) lies on the above curve.
-1 = [a(2) - b]/[(2 - 1)(2 - 4)]
-1 = (2a - b)/(-2)
⇒ 2a - b = 2 ...(i)
y = (ax - b)/(x² - 5x + 4)
differentiate w.r.t. x' on both sides,
y' = [a(1)(x² - 5x + 4) - (ax - b)(2x - 5)] / (x² - 5x + 4)²
Since, P(2, -1) is a turning point then y'|_{P(2,-1)} = 0
a[2² - 5(2) + 4] - (2a - b)(2(2) - 5) = 0
-2a - (2a - b)(-1) = 0
b = 0
Substitute b = 0 in Eq. (i)
2a - 0 = 2
a = 1.
name: Question Item 29

text: If the curved surface area of right circular cylinder inscribed in a sphere of radius 22 cm is maximum then height of the cylinder will be
explanation:
We have,
The C.S.A of a right circular cylinder inscribed in a sphere of radius R is maximum when height is √2 R
Height = √2 R = √2(22 cm)
= √2 (22/100 m) = 0.22√2 m.
name: Question Item 30

text: The radius of a sphere increases at the rate of 0.04 cm/sec. The rate of increase in the volume of that sphere with respect to its surface area, when its radius is 10 cm is
explanation:
Let r be the radius of the sphere.
Given, rate of change in radius dr/dt = 0.04 cm/sec
Volume of sphere (V) = (4/3)π r³
Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get
dV/dt = 4π r² dr/dt ...(i)
Surface area of sphere (S) = 4π r²
ds/dt = 8π r dr/dt ...(ii)
Eq. (i) divided by Eq. (ii), we get
dV/ds = r/2 = 10/2 [∵ r = 10 cm]
dV/ds = 5 cm.
name: Question Item 31

text: If f(x) = (2k+1)x - 3 - k e^{-x} + 2 e^x is monotonically increasing for all x ∈ ℝ, then the least value of k is
explanation:
Given,
f(x) = (2k+1)x - 3 - k e^{-x} + 2 e^x
Since, f(x) is monotonically increasing for all x ∈ ℝ.
So,
f'(x) ≥ 0
(2k+1) + k e^{-x} + 2 e^x ≥ 0
⇒ e^{-x}((2k+1)e^x + k + 2e^{2x}) ≥ 0
or
(2k+1)e^x + k + 2e^{2x} ≥ 0
⇒ 2e^x(e^x + k) + 1(e^x + k) ≥ 0
⇒ (2e^x + 1)(e^x + k) ≥ 0
⇒ e^x + k ≥ 0 or k ≥ 0
Hence, least value of k is zero.
name: Question Item 32

text: If f(x) = ax³ + bx² + 11x - 6 satisfies the conditions of Rolle's theorem in [1, 3] and f'(2 + 1/√3) = 0, then
explanation:
Given, f(x) = ax³ + bx² + 11x - 6 satisfies the Rolle's theorem in [1, 3]. So,
f(1) = f(3)
⇒ a + b + 5 = 0 ...(i)
and
27a + 9b + 27 = 0
⇒ 9a + 3b + 9 = 0
⇒ 3a + b + 3 = 0 ...(ii)
From Eq. (i), we get a + b = -5.
name: Question Item 33

text: If 2y = 3x - 1 is a tangent drawn to the curve y² = a x³ + b at (1, 1) where a, b are constants, then (a, b) =
explanation:
Given that 2y = 3x - 1 is tangent to y² = a x³ + b at point (1, 1) with slope 3/2.
Differentiate y² = a x³ + b w.r.t. x, we get
2y dy/dx = 3a x² ⇒ dy/dx = 3a x²/(2y)
⇒ (dy/dx)|_{(1,1)} = 3a(1)²/(2(1)) = 3a/2
Since, slope (dy/dx) = 3/2
∴ 3a/2 = 3/2 ⇒ a = 1
∴ Point (1, 1) lies on y² = a x³ + b
∴ (1)² = (1)(1)³ + b
⇒ 1 = 1 + b ⇒ b = 0.
name: Question Item 34

text: A ladder of 5 meters long rests against a vertical wall with the lower end on the horizontal ground. The lower end of the ladder is pulled along the ground away from the wall at the rate 3 m/sec. The height of the upper end (in meters) while it is descending at the rate of 4 m/sec, is
explanation:
In ΔOAP,
x² + y² = 5² ...(i)
and dx/dt = 3 m/sec, dy/dt = -4 m/s (since y is decreasing)
Differentiating Eq. (i) w.r.t. x, we get
2x dx/dt + 2y dy/dt = 0
⇒ x dx/dt + y dy/dt = 0
⇒ 3x - 4y = 0 [by Eq. (ii)]
⇒ 3x/4 = y
⇒ 3√(25 - y²) = 4y
By squaring both sides, we get
9(25 - y²) = 16y²
⇒ 225 - 9y² = 16y²
⇒ 25y² = 225 ⇒ y² = 9
⇒ y = 3 (∵ y ≥ 0).
name: Question Item 35

text: Suppose f''(x) exists for all real x. If f(2) = 2, f(3) = 5 and f(4) = 10, then which one among the following statements is definitely true?
explanation:
Let f(x) = a x² + b x + c
∴ f(2) ⇒ 4a + 2b + c = 2 ...(i)
f(3) ⇒ 9a + 3b + c = 5 ...(ii)
and f(4) ⇒ 16a + 4b + c = 10 ...(iii)
Subtracting Eq. (i) from Eq. (ii), we get
5a + b = 3 ...(iv)
Subtracting Eq. (ii) from Eq. (iii), we get
7a + b = 5 ...(v)
Subtracting Eq. (iv) from Eq. (v), we get
2a = 2 ⇒ a = 1
Put a = 1 in Eq. (iv), we get
5 × 1 + b = 3
⇒ b = 3 - 5 = -2
Put the value of a and b in Eq. (i),
4 - 4 + c = 2
⇒ c = 2
∴ f(x) = x² - 2x + 2
Now, f'(x) = 2x - 2
Again, f''(x) = 2
Since, f''(x) > 1 for some x ∈ (2, 4).
name: Question Item 36

text: If p and q are respectively the global maximum and global minimum of the function f(x) = x² e^{2x} on the interval [-2, 2], then p e^{-4} + q e^4 =
explanation:
Given, f(x) = x² e^{2x}
Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get
f'(x) = 2 e^{2x} x² + 2 x e^{2x} = 2 e^{2x} (x² + x)
⇒ f'(x) = 0 ⇒ 2 e^{2x} (x² + x) = 0
⇒ e^{2x} = 0 or x² + x = 0 ⇒ x = 0, -1
So, maxima and minima can be attained at either of -2, -1, 0, 2 as the function bound.
∴ f(-2) = (-2)² e^{-4} = 4 e^{-4}
f(-1) = (-1)² e^{-2}
f(0) = 0
f(2) = (2)² e^4 = 4 e^4
∴ p = 4 e^4 and q = 0
Therefore, p e^{-4} + q e^4 = 4 e^4 (e^{-4}) + 0 = 4 e^0
= 4 × 1 = 4.
name: Question Item 37

text: Tangents are drawn to the curve y = sin x from the origin. The locus of the points of contact is
explanation:
Given, y = sin x
Differentiating w.r.t x, we get
dy/dx = cos x
If tangent to y = sin x meet at (h, k)
(dy/dx)|_{(h,k)} = cos h
∴ Equation of tangent is cos h (x - h) = y - k
Since, tangent is passing through (origin).
∴ h cos h = k
But given curve is passing through (h, k)
k = sin h ⇒ cos h = √(1 - k²)
⇒ cos² h = 1 - k² = k²/h² ⇒ 1 - k²/h² = k²
⇒ h² - k² = h² k²
Hence, locus of point of contact is x² - y² = x² y².
name: Question Item 38

text: A man of height 2 m walks at a uniform speed of 7 m/min away from a lamp post of height 9 m. The rate (m/min) at which the length of his shadow increases is
explanation:
Let AB be the lamp-post and PQ the man, CP be his shadow at time t.
Let AP = x, PC = y, Also AB = 9 m, PQ = 2 m
Now, ΔCAB and ΔCPQ are equiangular and hence similar.
∴ PC/AC = PQ/AB
⇒ y/(x + y) = 2/9
⇒ 9y = 2x + 2y
⇒ 7y = 2x
⇒ x = (7/2)y
⇒ dx/dt = (7/2) dy/dt [differentiating w.r.t t]
But
dx/dt = 7 m/min
∴ 7 = (7/2) dy/dt
⇒ dy/dt = 2 m/min
∴ Length of shadow is increases at 2 m/min.
name: Question Item 39

text: The maximum volume (in cubic units) of the cylinder which can be inscribed in a sphere of diameter 6 units is
explanation:
If h is the height and V be the volume, required cylinder then from the given figure.
In ΔOAM,
r² + (h/2)² = (3)²
⇒ r² + h²/4 = 9 ⇒ r² = 9 - h²/4
Now, volume = π r² h = π (9 - h²/4) h
V = π (9h - h³/4) ⇒ dV/dh = (9 - 3h²/4) π
For maximum or minimum, dV/dh = 0
⇒ 9 - 3h²/4 = 0 ⇒ 3h²/4 = 9
⇒ h² = 12 ⇒ h = ± 2√3
∴ r² = 9 - 3 ⇒ r² = 6
The maximum value, [∵ d²V/dh² = -3h/2 < 0]
= π(6)(2√3) = 12√3 π sq units.
name: Question Item 40

text: The angle between the curves y² = 8(x + 4) and y² = 24(4 - x) is
explanation:
Given, curves are
y² = 8(x + 4) ...(i)
and y² = 24(4 - x) ...(ii)
By Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
8(x + 4) = 24(4 - x)
x + 4 = 12 - 3x
4x = 8 ⇒ x = 2
∴ y² = 8(6) ⇒ y = ± 4√3
For Eq. (i), 2y dy/dx = 8 ⇒ 2y m₁ = 8
m₁ = 8/(2y) ⇒ m₁ = 4/y = 4/(4√3) = 1/√3
For Eq. (ii), 2y dy/dx = -24 ⇒ y m₂ = -12
m₂ = -12/(4√3) = -√3
Here, m₁ m₂ = 1/√3 × -√3 = -1
So, angle between the curves tanθ = (m₁ - m₂)/(1 + m₁ m₂)
⇒ tanθ = (1/√3 - (-√3))/(1 + (-1)) ⇒ tanθ = ∞
⇒ tanθ = tan(π/2) ⇒ θ = π/2.
name: Question Item 41

text: The function f(x) = x^{1/x} for x > 0, is
options: increasing in (1, e) and decreasing in (e, ∞)
decreasing in (1, e) and increasing in (e, ∞)
explanation:
Given, f(x) = y = x^{1/x} ⇒ log y = (1/x) log x
Differentiate w.r.t. x, we get
(1/y) dy/dx = 1/x² - (log x)/x² ⇒ dy/dx = y [(1 - log x)/x²]
⇒ d/dx (x^{1/x}) = x^{1/x} [(1 - log x)/x²]
It is clear that, it is increasing in (1, e) and decreasing in (e, ∞).
name: Question Item 42

text: Let f be a polynomial function defined on [2, 7]. If f(2) = 3 and f'(x) ≤ 5 for all x in (2, 7), then the maximum possible value attained by f at x = 7 is
explanation:
Since, the polynomial function are continuous and differentiable in interval R.
So, according to Lagrange's mean value theorem
(f(7) - f(2))/(7 - 2) = f'(x) ≤ 5 (given)
⇒ f(7) - f(2) ≤ 25 ⇒ f(7) ≤ 28.
name: Question Item 43

text: In the interval [-2, 4], the absolute maximum of f(x) = 2x³ - 3x² - 12x + 5 occurs x =
explanation:
Given, function f(x) = 2x³ - 3x² - 12x + 5
So, f'(x) = 6x² - 6x - 12 = 0
[for maxima and minima f'(x) = 0]
⇒ x² - x - 2 = 0 ⇒ (x+1)(x-2) = 0
⇒ x = -1, 2 ∈ [-2, 4]
∴ f(-2) = -16 - 12 + 24 + 5 = 1
f(-1) = -2 - 3 + 12 + 5 = 12
f(2) = 16 - 12 - 24 + 5 = -15
and f(4) = 128 - 48 - 48 + 5 = 37
The absolute maximum of f(x) occurs at x = 4.
name: Question Item 44

text: If the radius of a sphere is mentioned as 7 m with an error of 0.02 m, then the approximate error in calculating its volume is
explanation:
Radius (r) = 7m
Error in radius (dr) = 0.02m
Volume of sphere (v) = (4/3)π r³
differentiate w.r.t. 'r' on both sides,
dv/dr = 4π r²
dv = 4π r² dr = 4π(49) · (0.02) = 3.92π m³.
name: Question Item 45

text: The tangent at the point (1, 2) to the curve y² = 4x makes an angle θ with the positive direction of X-axis. Then θ =
explanation:
y² = 4x; P = (1, 2)
differentiate w.r. to 'x'
2y · y' = 4 · 1 ⇒ y' = 4/(2y)
y' = 2/y
Slope of tangent = y'|_{P(1,2)} = 2/|y|_{P(1,2)}
tan θ = 1 ⇒ θ = 45°.
name: Question Item 46

text: A kind of bacteria grows by t³ in t s. Time taken for the rate of growth of the bacteria to become 1200 per s is ......
explanation:
Given,
dN/dt = t³,
When, rate of growth is 1200 we have,
d/dt (dN/dt) = 1200 ⇒ d/dt t³ = 1200
⇒ 3t² = 1200 ⇒ t² = 400
⇒ t = 20 sec.
name: Question Item 47

text: The sides of an equilateral triangle are increasing at the rate of 2 cm s⁻¹. How fast does its area increase when its side is 10 cm?
explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle A = (√3/4) a²
Differentiating with respect to time we get,
⇒ dA/dt = (√3/4) × 2a · da/dt
Here, da/dt = 2 cm⁻¹ when a = 10 cm
So, (dA/dt)|_{a=10 cm} = (√3/4) × 2 × 10 × 2 = 10√3 cm² · s⁻¹.
name: Question Item 48

text: The curve 3y² = 2a x² + 6b passes through the point P(3, -1) and the gradient of the curve at P is “-1”, then the values of a and b are
explanation:
Equation of given curve is
3y² = 2a x² + 6b ...(i)
∵ Curve (i) passes through point P(3, -1), so
3 = 18a + 6b
⇒ 6a + 2b = 1 ...(ii)
On differentiating the Eq. (i), we get
6y dy/dx = 4a x
⇒ 6 × (-1) × (-1) = 4a(3) ⇒ a = 1/2
∴ b = -1.
name: Question Item 49

text: The value of 'k' for which the function f(x) = k(x + sin x) + k is increasing, is equal to
explanation:
Given function f(x) = k(x + sin x) + k
So, f'(x) = k(1 + cos x)
∵ f(x) is an increasing function, so
f'(x) ≥ 0
⇒ k(1 + cos x) ≥ 0
⇒ k > 0 {∵ 1 + cos x ≥ 0}.
name: Question Item 50
